pi_items.source: setup_task.sh Python script generates this file by creating Blender primitives (line 94-95 creates output_path)
Task description explicitly states: Save the project to '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/jelly_sim.blend'
setup_task.sh line 53 explicitly sets: jelly.name = "JellySuzanne"; also confirmed in task description
setup_task.sh line 37 explicitly sets: plate.name = "Plate"; also confirmed in task description

Task description (line 6) explicitly states: 'reduce strength (Default) to between 0.4 and 0.8'; verifier.py line 85 checks '0.3 <= goal <= 0.9', but this contradicts the task description requirement of 0.4-0.8

Task description (line 6) explicitly states: 'reduce strength (Default) to between 0.4 and 0.8'; verifier.py line 85 checks '0.3 <= goal <= 0.9', but this contradicts the task description requirement of 0.4-0.8
Task description (line 7) requires: 'Ensure Bending is > 0.5 for structural integrity'; metadata matches this value, but verifier.py does not validate the bend value despite extracting it (line 80)
install_blender.sh line 90 specifies BLENDER_VERSION="4.2.4"; downloaded from https://download.blender.org/release/Blender4.2/blender-4.2.4-linux-x64.tar.xz
setup_blender.sh lines 19-20 create config for blender version 4.2, matching the installed Blender 4.2.4

privileged_info_summary: The task uses Blender 4.2.4 LTS (from official download). The scene contains two objects: 'JellySuzanne' (a subdivided monkey/Suzanne primitive at z=3.0) and 'Plate' (a cylinder at z=-0.1, radius=2.0). The expected output is saved to /home/ga/BlenderProjects/jelly_sim.blend. The task requires Goal strength between 0.4-0.8, but the verifier checks 0.3-0.9 (contradiction with task description). A stiffness_threshold of 0.5 is specified for Bending, though the verifier does not enforce this. All data is synthetic: objects are Blender primitives with programmatically generated materials (CeramicWhite and StrawberryJelly), with no external datasets or real-world data used.
